title: sphaeralcea incana var. typica
intro: Sphaeralcea incana var. typica is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae) and commonly known as Gray Desert Marigold or Desert Marigold. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, colds, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea incana var. typica include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
